channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This money-saving emporium is like Facebook Marketplace for gift cards
item.description: You don’t have to be an avid reader of restaurant industry trade publications—though I can attest that they are oddly fascinating—to realize that everything’s getting more expensive. The good news is that there’s an easy way to counteract those rising menu prices.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Your latest prescription is to get outside
item.description: Health care providers are trying a new tactic to promote the healing effects of nature: They're actually writing prescriptions for it.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: If the new Prime Video movie Hedda reminds you of Saltburn, you’re not the only one – but the cast don’t see it
item.description: Is the new movie Hedda really the next Saltburn? The cast ‘don’t see it,’ but you still can stream both on Prime Video.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Valentine, TechRadar

item.title: What Hollywood’s next potential merger means for streaming
item.description: One of Hollywood’s crown jewels is on the block: Warner Bros. Discovery, the parent company of HBO, CNN, and major movie franchises like Harry Potter and the DC Universe, officially confirmed this week that it is open to a sale.
item.dc:creator: Janko Roettgers, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists spot skyscraper-sized asteroid racing through solar system
item.description: Astronomers have discovered asteroid 2025 SC79, a skyscraper-sized space rock orbiting the sun in just 128 days. the second-fastest known.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sora is even fooling human deepfake detectors
item.description: It used to be that artificial intelligence would leave behind helpful clues that an image it produced was not, in fact, real. Previous generations of the technology might give a person an extra finger or even an additional limb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Google TPUs find sweet spot of AI demand, a decade after chip’s debut
item.description: In an AI chip industry that's almost entirely commanded by Nvidia Corp., a Google chip first developed more than 10 years ago especially for artificial intelligence tasks is finally gaining momentum outside its home company as a way to train and run complex AI models.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel rallies after forecast signals a comeback Is underway
item.description: Intel Corp. shares jumped after the chipmaker returned to profitability and gave an upbeat revenue forecast, suggesting that it's making progress on a long and challenging comeback attempt.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Why Nvidia just invested in a battery recycling company
item.description: As Nvidia’s value has soared—becoming the first public company to hit $4 trillion in market capitalization earlier this year—it’s been pouring money into AI startups. Its venture arm, NVentures, is also backing less expected bets.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple may turn off key privacy tool in Europe
item.description: In a victory for surveillance capitalism, Apple may be forced to leave its users in Europe vulnerable to rapacious ad data collection in response to “intense lobbying” by politicians in the region.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Bay Area donors that are helping pay for $300 million White House ballroom
item.description: Trump has said the ballroom will be paid for by himself and donors, with no taxpayer money being used.
item.dc:creator: Bay Area News Group, The Mercury News

item.title: Anthropic signs billion-dollar deal with Google Cloud
item.description: US-based AI company Anthropic has signed a major deal with Google Cloud that is said to be worth tens of billions of dollars. As part of the deal, Anthropic will have access to up to one million of Google’s purpose-built Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) AI accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA warns Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager has a worrying security flaw, so patch now
item.description: The critical severity bug is being actively exploited in the wild, possibly by ransomware actors, CISA says.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Spotify gives parents new power to control what their kids hear on streaming platform
item.description: Spotify's managed accounts for kids under 13 now available in at least seven countries, allowing parents to filter and block explicit content and songs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nissan adds solar roof to Japan's most popular little EV – and it promises to add 1,800 miles of free range a year
item.description: An extendable solar roof can add useable mileage to Nissan's diminutive EV and the tech is going into production.
item.dc:creator: Leon Poultney, TechRadar

item.title: Intel posts profit even as it struggles to regain market share
item.description: Intel posted profits in the latest quarter as the one-time America technology icon struggles to regain market share.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Condon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scammers are targeting cloud systems to make off with hauls of gift cards
item.description: Gift cards are ideal for hackers since they can easily be redeemed or sold without sharing personally identifiable data.
item.dc:creator: Sead Fadilpašić, TechRadar

item.title: Musk's rollercoaster year: From boycotts and plunging earnings to a potential trillion-dollar payday
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If someone left a government job with a black eye, literally, ran a company with shrinking profits, and suddenly had federal investigators crawling over their business, you might say they’re having a bad year. But most people are not Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pardons Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, high-profile cryptocurrency figure
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, who created the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange and served prison time for failing to stop criminals from using the platform to move money connected to child sex abuse, drug trafficking and terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Reddit sues Perplexity, three other firms, for AI scraping
item.description: Reddit this week filed suit against Perplexity and three other companies — Oxylabs UAB, AWM Proxy, and Serp Api — for allegedly engaging in so-called AI scraping without authorization.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Dinosaurs were thriving in North America before the mass-extinction asteroid strike, study suggests
item.description: New research suggests that dinosaur populations were thriving in North America before the asteroid impact 66 million years ago that caused mass extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI is about to upend Google’s AdWords cash cow
item.description: Twenty-five years ago, Google unveiled Adwords, which pledged to enable advertisers “to quickly design a flexible program that best fits [their] online marketing goals and budget,” Google cofounder Larry Page said at the time. The principle was simple.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: American e-waste is causing a 'hidden tsunami' in Southeast Asia, report says
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Millions of tons of discarded electronics from the United States are being shipped overseas, much of it to developing countries in Southeast Asia unprepared to safely handle hazardous waste, according to a new report released Wednesday by an environmental watchdog.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside Google’s multi-architecture revolution: Axion Arm joins x86 in production clusters
item.description: While x86 has been dominant for decades, a new migration project at Google represents a significant shift to more mixed architectures.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: PyTorch team unveils framework for programming clusters
item.description: The PyTorch team at Meta, stewards of the PyTorch open source machine learning framework, has unveiled Monarch, a distributed programming framework intended to bring the simplicity of PyTorch to entire clusters.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Serious vulnerability found in Rust library
item.description: Developers creating projects in the Rust programming language, as well as IT leaders with Rust-based applications in their environments, should pay attention to a serious vulnerability found in one of the programming language’s libraries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Reddit sues AI company Perplexity and others for 'industrial-scale' scraping of user comments
item.description: Social media platform Reddit sued the artificial intelligence company Perplexity AI and three other entities on Wednesday, alleging their involvement in an “industrial-scale, unlawful” economy to “scrape” the comments of millions of Reddit users for commercial gain.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kevin O’Leary says ‘Marty Supreme’ blew millions by not using AI actors
item.description: The “Shark Tank” star said the production costs are outdated when AI can create realistic looking background characters.
item.dc:creator: Teddy Grant, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI launches Atlas browser to compete with Google Chrome
item.description: OpenAI introduced its own web browser, Atlas, on Tuesday, putting the ChatGPT maker in direct competition with Google as more internet users rely on artificial intelligence to answer their questions.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Meghan Markle, Prince Harry join call for AI 'superintelligence' ban
item.description: The couple is among more than 700 public figures to sign on to a brief statement by the Future of Life Institute.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine unveils upgraded sea drone it says can strike anywhere in the Black Sea
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine's state security service has unveiled an upgraded sea drone it says can now operate anywhere in the Black Sea, carry heavier weapons and use artificial intelligence for targeting.
item.dc:creator: EFREM LUKATSKY and DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
